risk: Cross-tenant data exposure (RLS bypass / IDOR across firm_id)
existing_controls: Postgres RLS on every domain table; asUser executes as role authenticated; npm run test:rls in CI; superadmin allowlist default-deny

risk: Credential compromise of an advisor or admin account
existing_controls: Clerk auth; TOTP MFA required for advisor/admin; 30-min idle timeout; rate limiting

risk: Exposure or loss of EB_DOCUMENT_KEY / EB_SIGNING_KEY
existing_controls: Keys in host secret store, never in git; prod hard-fails without them; AES-256-GCM at rest
Reputable providers with own SOC 2/ISO; stateless redeployable compute; managed DB w/ PITR

risk: Data loss or corruption in the primary database
existing_controls: Supabase managed backups + point-in-time recovery; immutable assessment snapshots; code/config in git

risk: Untested recovery — restore fails or exceeds RTO when needed
Malicious or accidental insider access to client data
existing_controls: Least privilege; service-role confined to trusted operations; audit log (data_access_log); offboarding checklist
Parameterized SQL; React auto-escaping; safe content-type serving; PR review + CI; source review found no high/critical findings
npm audit currently 0 vulns; dependency updates via PR
Optional ClamAV scanning (EB_SCANNER=clamav); documents encrypted + served as attachments with nosniff

risk: No SOC 2 report / no pen test blocks enterprise/RIA sales
existing_controls: Readiness assessment + policy pack (this effort); pen test scoped
Offboarding checklist (policy 14): revoke Clerk, rotate shared secrets, remove repo/cloud access

risk: No background checks before handling client NPI
existing_controls: Background-check policy drafted (planned before first enterprise engagement)
IR plan (policy 04); committed 30-day/72-hour target; contract clause being finalized

risk: Client data leaked via logs or error monitoring
existing_controls: Sentry seam scrubs secrets/PII; policy: no NPI in logs; audit log stores metadata not payloads
Architecture rule 1: no LLM writes to scoring tables; scores verified against reference_scorer.py in CI; AI output labeled draft

risk: Structured NPI (financials, assessment answers, scores, PII) is not field-level encrypted, so a database-layer compromise (leaked DB credential or provider-side read) exposes it as plaintext
existing_controls: Supabase-managed volume encryption at rest; RLS tenant isolation; TLS in transit; secrets in host stores not code; uploaded documents carry an additional app-level AES-256-GCM layer whose key lives outside the data plane
